name: Cream of Poblano Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the corn tortillas into ninths and place in a food processor. Chop until fine.
2. Add flour, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper to the food processor and blend until the mixture reaches the consistency of cornmeal.
3. Heat canola oil in a stockpot over medium-high heat.
4. Add onions, peppers, and garlic to the pot. Saute until onions are clear.
5. Add butter and let it melt.
6. Add the tortilla-flour mixture to the pan and mix to form a roux, stirring with a wire whip. Do not let it burn.
7. Slowly add chicken stock while continuously stirring, scraping the sides and bottom of the pot.
8. Add half-and-half and bring the soup to a slow simmer for 7-10 minutes. Do not allow the soup to come to a hard boil.
9. Turn off heat and let the soup cool.
10. Before serving, add roasted chopped chicken to the stockpot.
11. Top each serving with shredded Monterrey Jack cheese, diced poblano peppers, and crisply fried tortilla strips.
